text: Prove where/how much recurrent mode hurts transformer quality: full-KV vs ring vs ring+bank on gemma-4-E4B, depth-stratified synthetic grid + position-resolved NLL

title: Build quality_eval.py harness (grid + nll subcommands, 3 modes, smoke mode)
check: cd /home/xiaol/X/wkvm && HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 /home/xiaol/X/HRM-Text/.venv/bin/python experiments/quality_eval.py grid --smoke 2>&1 | grep -q GRID_OK

title: Run depth-stratified synthetic grid (needle single/multi-key/aggregation x ctx 8k/16k/32k x depth bins x modes)
Position-resolved NLL on natural long docs, 3 modes, position-binned deltas
Analysis doc + COMPARISON update + commit/push
check: cd /home/xiaol/X/wkvm && git log --oneline -1 | grep -qi 'quality' && test -f docs/RECURRENT_MODE_QUALITY.md

text: smoke preview: with NON-repetitive filler, banked mode already misses the evicted-depth needle at ctx2048/depth0.2 (ring also 0.00, full 1.00; in-ring depth 0.8 all modes 1.00). PoC-2's 18/18 recall was flattered by repetitive filler as suspected.

grid done, 45 rows: full=1.00 on t1/t2 everywhere incl 32k(chunk1024, no OOM), t3~0.83 (model's own aggregation ceiling). ring=0.00 at every evicted depth, recalls only in-ring (8k depth0.9). banked with honest non-repetitive filler: t1 recovers well but imperfect (0.33-1.00, mean~0.75), t2 collapses at 16k/32k mid depths (0.00 at 16k depths 0.1-0.7; distractor facts compete for rep slots through merge chain), t3 worst (0.00-0.67). One inversion: t3@8k depth0.9 banked 0.17 < ring 0.50.

nll done, 6 docs x 16k (2 tex, 2 md, 2 py): bins 0-2k exact (delta 0.0000, chunked-eviction granularity makes divergence start at 2k). ring delta vs full grows +0.32@2-3k to +0.81@15-16k (peak +1.36@12-13k, high per-doc std 0.38-0.83). banked recovers only ~0.05-0.13 nats of the ring gap everywhere (e.g. +0.61 vs +0.74 @4-5k) - the bank helps targeted recall, barely helps general LM. NO NLL@8k-style inversion on natural text: both lossy modes strictly worse than full past the window, confirming PoC-2's ppl inversion was repetitive-filler artifact.

title: Implement training-free routed bank (online key-cluster routing, leader replay) as 4th mode in PoC + quality_eval
check: cd /home/xiaol/X/wkvm && HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 /home/xiaol/X/HRM-Text/.venv/bin/python experiments/quality_eval.py grid --smoke --modes full,ring,banked,routed 2>&1 | grep -q GRID_OK

title: Run grid + NLL with routed column (variant sweep, best honest variant reported)
check: grep -c 'routed' experiments/results/quality_grid.md | awk '{exit !($1>=15)}' && grep -q NLL_CURVE_OK experiments/results/quality_nll.md

title: Routed-bank analysis in RECURRENT_MODE_QUALITY.md + commit/push
check: cd /home/xiaol/X/wkvm && git log --oneline -1 | grep -qi routed && grep -qi 'routed bank' docs/RECURRENT_MODE_QUALITY.md

text: training-free routing only (user constraint): online key-clustering router, no learned parameters; learned router deferred

variant sweep (t1/t2@8k, means): routed-value-m64 0.625 > routed-value-m16 0.50 > banked 0.375 > routed-key-m64 = routed-resid-m64 0.25 > routed-key-m16 = routed-resid-m16 0.00. VALUE routing (no RoPE) is the only feature that beats temporal banking; raw/residual KEY clustering is position-contaminated and at M=16 loses the needle entirely (worse than banked). Residual-mean subtraction did NOT rescue key routing. Finalists for full grid: routed-value-m16, routed-value-m64.

full grid 5 modes done: routed-value-m64 overall 0.79 vs banked 0.45, ring 0.06, full 0.95. Per task: t1 1.00 (holds banked's 0.80->1.00), t2 0.51 (vs banked 0.33; at 32k it scores 0.87 avg incl 1.00 cells - content routing does separate the template-sharing facts when M=64), t3 0.86 = full's own ceiling. routed-value-m16 (banked's budget class): t1 1.00, t3 0.38, but t2 0.29 ~ banked. NLL: routed-value-m64 delta +0.10@2-3k -> +0.84@15-16k, ~0.1nat better than banked in early bins, converges to ring/banked deep; still a recall device, not LM repair. NLL_CURVE_OK, bins 0-2k exact for all 4 lossy modes.
